currentUse: Active Roman Catholic worship site of the merged Immaculate Conception – Five Holy Martyrs Parish (Archdiocese of Chicago), 2745 W. 44th Street, Brighton Park; regular Spanish, bilingual, and Lithuanian Masses
summary: The 1964 modernist Immaculate Conception church at 2745 W. 44th Street in Brighton Park is standing and in active Catholic use as one of two worship sites of the merged Immaculate Conception – Five Holy Martyrs Parish, formed in 2019 under the Archdiocese of Chicago's Renew My Church restructuring; the property remains in diocesan hands and was never sold. The parish's own history page states it was established in 1914 by Lithuanian immigrants and, despite the community becoming predominantly Hispanic, "retains its Lithuanian heritage." The current parish website's Mass schedule lists a Sunday 10:00 am Lithuanian-language Mass at the Immaculate Conception site, alongside Spanish Masses there and English/Polish/Spanish Masses at the Five Holy Martyrs site (4327 S. Richmond St.). The parish history page names Rev. Miguel Venegas as pastor from 2020 to present. The building keeps extensive Lithuanian fabric — an Our Lady of Šiluva shrine and a Šiluva stained-glass window in Lithuanian flag colors, Lithuanian donor names on windows, and a traditional Lithuanian wayside cross erected in 1987 — while the surrounding parish population has been majority-Hispanic since the 1980s. Chicago's Lithuanian community organizations marked the parish's 110th anniversary with a jubilee Mass and reception in fall 2024. No closure, sale, or new restructuring threat to either worship site was found in searches through July 2026.

developments.headline: Merged with Five Holy Martyrs under Renew My Church; both churches kept as worship sites
developments.detail: The parish's official history page states that in 2019 Immaculate Conception consolidated with the Polish Five Holy Martyrs parish "as part of the Chicago Archdiocese Renew My Church Grouping," creating a single united parish. Both church buildings remain active worship sites of the merged parish, each with its own Mass schedule.

sources.title: About Us — Immaculate Conception-Five Holy Martyrs Parish
sources.publisher: Immaculate Conception – Five Holy Martyrs Parish

developments.headline: Rev. Miguel Venegas installed as pastor of the merged parish
developments.detail: The parish history page lists Rev. Miguel Venegas as pastor from 2020 to present, the ninth pastoral leader in the parish's history and the first of the merged entity's settled pastorate after unification.

sources.title: About Us — Immaculate Conception-Five Holy Martyrs Parish
sources.publisher: Immaculate Conception – Five Holy Martyrs Parish

developments.headline: Lithuanian community celebrates the parish's 110th anniversary
developments.detail: Chicago Lithuanian organizations (LAC Brighton Park Chapter, Chicago Lithuanian Center–Jaunimo Centras, Draugas newspaper, Lithuanian Foundation, LAC Chicago Chapter) organized a 110th-anniversary jubilee Mass at the church followed by a reception at the Chicago Lithuanian Center in fall 2024. Note: the source event page (sauliai.org) was unreachable on direct fetch (HTTP 503); details rest on search-index snippets of that page, and reported event dates vary between September 29 and October 6, 2024.

sources.title: Celebrating 110 Years of Faith and Fellowship — Immaculate Conception Parish

developments.headline: Weekly Lithuanian Mass continues at the Immaculate Conception site
developments.detail: The merged parish's current website lists the Immaculate Conception worship site schedule as Sunday 7:00 am Spanish, 10:00 am Lithuanian, 12:00 pm Spanish, plus weekday and Saturday Spanish/bilingual Masses; Five Holy Martyrs keeps Sunday English, Polish, and Spanish Masses. No announcement of closure or further restructuring of either site was found.

sources.title: Chicago, Illinois: Lithuanians and Lithuanian heritage
Celebrating 110 Years of Faith and Fellowship — Immaculate Conception Parish

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions. Findings confirm every archive fact: merged 2019 with Five Holy Martyrs into a multi-ethnic parish, both buildings open, a separate Sunday Lithuanian Mass (10:00 am) continues, ownership remains diocesan, building standing. Refinements: the merged parish operates as "Immaculate Conception – Five Holy Martyrs Parish" with Rev. Miguel Venegas as pastor since 2020; the standing church is the 1964 modernist building (the parish's second church); the parish community has been majority-Hispanic since the 1980s, with the Lithuanian presence now one liturgy and heritage fabric (Šiluva shrine, flag-colored stained glass, 1987 wayside cross) rather than a demographic majority.
gaps: The archdiocesan announcement articles (chicagocatholic.com, 2018 and 2019) returned HTTP 403, and the Renew My Church decision PDF was a scanned image with no extractable text, so the widely reported July 1, 2019 effective date of the merger could not be confirmed from a directly opened source — only "2019" is verified via the parish's own history page. The sauliai.org 110th-anniversary event page returned HTTP 503; the celebration is attested in search-index snippets from two independent queries, but the exact date is inconsistent across them (Sept 29 vs Oct 6, 2024). The current pastor listing (Rev. Miguel Venegas, 2020–present) comes from the parish about-us page, which may be stale; the ic5hm.org homepage does not name a pastor, so his tenure as of mid-2026 is not independently confirmed. No press about preservation campaigns, property valuation, or restructuring exposure for either building was found in searches through July 2026 — absence of such news, not confirmation of safety.
